senses.definition: a vertical pipe attached to a building which carries away rainwater from the roof to the ground

examples.x: The heavy rain caused the downspout to overflow, leading to water pooling around the foundation.
He climbed up the ladder to clean out the leaves blocking the downspout.
The downspout was rusted, so we had to replace it with a new plastic one.
She noticed that the downspout was broken, causing the rainwater to spill over the sides.
The builder installed a downspout at each corner of the house to ensure proper water flow.

senses.definition: a system of underground pipes and tunnels used to carry away used water and waste matter from houses, factories, etc.

examples.x: Raw sewage leaked into the river due to a malfunction in the sewer network.
Urban planners designed the new neighborhood with a modern sewer to accommodate population growth.
The sewer inspector checked for cracks and leaks in the aging infrastructure to prevent contamination.

senses.definition: the pipes of a building or machine that carry water, gas, or oil
the obstruction or partial restriction of a passage, such as a pipe, duct, or pathway, preventing the smooth flow of air, fluid, or other substances
an underground tank used for the temporary storage of sewage or wastewater

examples.x: The house was built in a rural area, so it relies on a cesspit for waste storage.
They had to call a waste removal service to empty the full cesspit in the backyard.
The old property had a cesspit instead of a modern septic system.
After heavy rainfall, the cesspit began to overflow, causing a mess in the garden.
The construction team had to dig a new cesspit after the old one became too small for the growing property.

senses.definition: a valve, gate, or device that is used to stop or control the flow of fluid or gas within a pipe or conduit
a pipe in the bottom of a sink, bath, etc. through which dirty water flows out

examples.x: The plumber unclogged the drain in the kitchen sink, allowing water to flow freely again.
The shower drain was blocked with hair and soap scum, causing water to pool at the bottom of the tub.
Residents were advised to avoid pouring grease down the drain to prevent clogs in the plumbing system.
A mesh screen was installed over the drain to catch debris and prevent it from entering the pipes.

senses.definition: the process of removing excess water or other liquids from an area or system, typically through a network of pipes, channels, or natural slopes

examples.x: Proper drainage is essential for maintaining the health of the garden and preventing waterlogging.
The local government is focusing on improving the drainage in areas prone to flooding during the rainy season.
The contractor ensured that the drainage around the building was designed to avoid any water damage.

senses.definition: a pipe installed in a building's plumbing system that carries wastewater or excess water away from the structure
a hollow conduit or passage that is used for the distribution, circulation, or ventilation of air, gases, or other substances within a building or system
the system of pipes, channels, or conduits used to convey or distribute air, gases, or other substances within a building, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system, or industrial setup

examples.x: The technician inspected the ducting to ensure there were no leaks in the HVAC system.
The air conditioning system was upgraded with better ducting to improve airflow throughout the office.
He noticed that the ducting in the old house was clogged, which was affecting the airflow.
The company is considering replacing the outdated ducting with a more energy-efficient option.

senses.definition: a large congealed mass or blockage in a sewer system that is primarily composed of fats, oils, grease, and non-biodegradable items that have been flushed down drains or toilets

examples.x: A large fatberg caused a major blockage in the city's sewer system, leading to flooding in some areas.
The sewer maintenance crew discovered a massive fatberg that was obstructing the main drain.
Many cities have launched campaigns to educate people on the dangers of fatbergs and how to prevent them.
It took several days and specialized equipment to remove the fatberg from the pipes.

senses.definition: an object that controls the flow of liquid or gas from a container or pipe

examples.x: He turned on the faucet to fill the pot with water.
The faucet in the bathroom was leaking, so a plumber was called.
She replaced the old faucet with a modern, stainless steel one.
Make sure to turn off the faucet completely to save water.
The outdoor faucet was used to connect the garden hose.

senses.definition: an open pipe that is attached beneath the edge of a building roof and carries rainwater away

examples.x: The gutter along the roofline prevented rainwater from dripping onto the walkway below.
They cleaned out the leaves and debris clogging the gutter to prevent water damage.
The downspout carried water from the gutter down to the ground.
The gutter overflowed during heavy rainfall, causing water damage to the foundation.
She heard the sound of rainwater rushing through the gutter during the storm.

senses.definition: a large pipe through which water or gas is carried to a building

examples.x: The main was shut off to prevent further damage to the water system.
The building is connected to the city's main for water supply.
They traced the issue back to a fault in the electrical main.

senses.definition: a point or location where water or another substance is discharged or flows out from a system, such as a drainage system, sewer, or wastewater treatment plant, into a body of water or the environment

examples.x: The outfall of the stormwater system is located near the edge of the river.
The workers are repairing the outfall to prevent any water from spilling into the nearby park.
After heavy rain, the outfall released a large amount of water into the sea.
The city installed a new outfall to improve drainage in the downtown area.
The environmental group is monitoring the outfall for signs of pollution.

senses.definition: a network of pipes, channels, and structures designed to collect and channel excess water, wastewater, or other liquids away from an area or a property

examples.x: Workers are repairing the drainage system to ensure it functions properly.
A blocked drainage system can cause water to back up into the streets.
Proper maintenance of the drainage system is crucial for avoiding water damage.
After the storm, the drainage system worked well, keeping the area dry.

senses.definition: the unintended or accidental escape or loss of a substance, typically a liquid or gas, from a container, system, or structure

examples.x: The plumber fixed the leakage in the pipe under the kitchen sink.
We noticed a small leakage in the roof after the heavy rain.
The leakage from the water tank caused damage to the floor.
They are investigating the leakage in the factory’s cooling system.

senses.definition: a cylindrical hollow tube or conduit typically made of metal, plastic, or other materials, used for the transportation of fluids, gases, or other substances from one place to another

examples.x: The plumber installed a new pipe to ensure the water flowed smoothly.
The gas company replaced the old pipe to prevent leaks.
The factory used a series of pipes to transport chemicals safely.
They discovered a leak in the pipe that carried water to the entire building.
The oil flowed through the underground pipe to the refinery.

senses.definition: a system of interconnected pipes used for the transport of liquids, gases, or other substances over long distances
water that is brought into a house, building, etc. through pipes
an underground container that treats household wastewater by allowing solids to settle and separate from the liquid before further treatment or absorption into the soil

examples.x: The plumber came to check if the septic tank needed to be emptied.
They had to install a new septic tank because the old one was too damaged.
The house relies on a septic tank for waste disposal since it is in a rural area.
After the heavy rain, they noticed the septic tank was starting to overflow.
The family decided to upgrade their septic tank to a larger one to accommodate their growing household.

senses.definition: the waste water and other liquid waste from homes, businesses, and factories, usually carried away through pipes and treated

examples.x: The treatment plant processes millions of gallons of sewage daily.
Proper disposal of sewage is essential for public health.
A broken sewage line disrupted water services in the neighborhood.
Improper handling of sewage can lead to the spread of diseases.

senses.definition: a channel or gate that is used to control or redirect the flow of water. It typically consists of a barrier that can be opened or closed to regulate the passage of water
a valve or tap that is used to control the release of water from a pipe or container
a vertical pipe or fixture connected to a water supply system, typically equipped with valves or outlets, used for firefighting purposes or as a source of water in buildings

examples.x: The firefighter connected the hose to the standpipe to quickly access water during the emergency.
The building's standpipe system was tested to make sure it could handle high water pressure in case of a fire.
The workers turned the valve on the standpipe to begin filling the water tank.
The standpipe outside the warehouse provided a backup water supply during the drought.
The standpipe on the street corner was used to wash cars and clean the sidewalk.

senses.definition: a valve or faucet used to control or shut off the flow of water or other fluids in a plumbing system, typically located at a point where pipes connect or branch

examples.x: The plumber turned off the stopcock to fix the leaky pipe under the sink.
If there is a burst pipe, you should immediately close the stopcock to stop the water flow.
The landlord showed me where the stopcock is located in case of an emergency.
We had to replace the old stopcock because it was leaking water.
Before beginning any plumbing work, make sure to turn off the stopcock to avoid flooding.

senses.definition: a mechanical device that controls or regulates the flow of fluids, such as liquids, gases, or slurries, by opening, closing, or partially obstructing passageways within a pipe or system

examples.x: The valve was turned to stop the water from leaking.
She adjusted the valve to control the airflow in the lab.
The valve near the pump regulates the fuel flow.
We need to close the valve to prevent flooding in the basement.

senses.definition: a pit or reservoir typically found in the basement or lower level of a building that collects excess water or liquid, such as groundwater, rainwater, or wastewater

a pipe within a building's plumbing system that carries wastewater from plumbing fixtures, such as sinks, toilets, or showers, to the main drainage system or septic tank for proper disposal or treatment

examples.x: The plumber fixed the clogged waste pipe under the sink.
Waste pipes are connected to the drainage system to carry away wastewater.
A broken waste pipe can cause flooding in the basement.
The kitchen’s waste pipe needs to be cleaned regularly to prevent blockages.
Water from the shower drains into a waste pipe that leads outside.

senses.definition: a large underground pipe that delivers potable water from a water treatment plant or other water source to individual buildings or communities

examples.x: A leak in the water main caused water to spill onto the sidewalk.
The water main broke, causing a temporary water outage in the neighborhood.
The city is replacing old water mains to improve the water supply system.
The workers are digging up the street to repair the damaged water main.

senses.definition: the infrastructure and facilities responsible for supplying clean water and managing wastewater
the system of pipes for the distribution of water in a building

examples.x: The plumbing in the old house needed extensive repairs due to leaks and corrosion.
The plumber installed new fixtures as part of the plumbing renovation in the bathroom.
He learned the basics of plumbing to be able to handle minor repairs around the house.
The apartment complex experienced a plumbing emergency when a pipe burst in the basement.

senses.definition: a device or appliance connected to a plumbing system that is used for a specific function related to water supply or drainage

examples.x: The plumber installed a new plumbing fixture in the kitchen for the sink.
She replaced the old plumbing fixtures in the bathroom with modern ones.
The house was renovated, and all the plumbing fixtures were upgraded.
The plumbing fixture in the shower was leaking, so it needed to be repaired.
When building a house, it is important to select plumbing fixtures that suit both style and function.

senses.definition: a component used in plumbing and construction to join, redirect, or control the flow of fluids or gases in a piping system

examples.x: The plumber used a pipe fitting to connect the two sections of pipe under the sink.
A leak developed because one of the pipe fittings was not installed correctly.
He went to the hardware store to buy the right pipe fitting for the outdoor irrigation system.
The contractor used several pipe fittings to complete the plumbing for the new house.

senses.definition: a plumbing fixture specifically designed for the purpose of urination. It is typically found in public restrooms and is designed to be used by males

examples.x: The janitor was busy cleaning the urinals in the men's bathroom.
There was a long line for the toilets, but the urinals were empty.
After the game, the men's restroom had a long line for the urinals.
The stadium installed new urinals to accommodate the large crowd during events.

senses.definition: a large and open container that has a water supply and you can use to wash your hands, dishes, etc. in

examples.x: She filled the sink with warm, soapy water to wash the dirty dishes.
He rinsed his hands under the running water in the sink after gardening.
The bathroom sink had a leak, so they called a plumber to fix it.
They installed a deep farmhouse sink in the kitchen for washing large pots and pans.
The utility sink in the laundry room was perfect for soaking stained clothing.

senses.definition: a type of sink that is specifically designed for use in laundry rooms or areas where clothes are washed or laundered

examples.x: The laundry sink is where I wash delicate items that cannot go in the washing machine.
After cleaning the garage, he used the laundry sink to wash his hands.
She filled the laundry sink with water to wash the muddy shoes.
The laundry sink is perfect for soaking stained clothes overnight.

senses.definition: the seat we use for getting rid of bodily waste

examples.x: The toilet in the modern bathroom featured sleek design and water-efficient technology.
A clogged toilet led to a humorous yet embarrassing situation during the family gathering.
The bathroom remodel included a state-of-the-art toilet with built-in bidet functions for added comfort.
The children learned the importance of proper toilet etiquette during their potty training phase.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment that flows water all over your body from above

a type of pipe fitting that has external threads on the outer surface, designed to be inserted into a corresponding female pipe fitting with internal threads to create a secure and sealed connection for the flow of fluids or gases

a type of pipe fitting that has internal threads on the inner surface, designed to receive and make a secure connection with a male pipe fitting that has external threads, allowing for the flow of fluids or gases
